title: Tilia pseudorubra
intro:
There is no information available on Tilia pseudorubra. However, there is information on Tilia amurensis which is known as Tilia pseudorubra by some sources. 

Tilia amurensis is a deciduous tree species, belonging to the family Malvaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues.

The bioactive constituents of Tilia amurensis include flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol), phenolic acids (gallic acid, caffeic acid), and terpenoids (tilofuran, limonene), which contribute to its therapeutic properties.

Tilia amurensis is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, often combined with other medicinal plants to create remedies for anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Tilia pseudorubra is classified within the genus Tilia, which is a part of the family Malvaceae. This genus is further classified within the order Malvales and the clade Rosids. Tilia pseudorubra is a member of the tribe Tilioideae and the subfamily Tilioideae. It is native to China and is known for its distinct characteristics. The classification of Tilia pseudorubra reflects its unique position within the plant kingdom.
